title: Analytical Methodology — eleven discipline pages from a Georgetown Applied Intelligence curriculum, treating each analytical method as its own working surface

fields.vision: A Georgetown Applied Intelligence methodology pillar that treats eleven analytical disciplines as distinct working surfaces.
fields.mechanism: It differentiates all-source, counterintelligence, crisis management, counter-operations, cyber, defense/diplomacy/intelligence, emerging-threats, foresight, homeland-security, intelligence-cycle, and public-service analysis by case anchor, source demand, method fit, output form, and cross-method composition. Meridian Network, Operation GRAY DAWN, the Caracas Question, and HS-style signal-detection moves function as worked anchors rather than empty labels.
fields.built_components: The eleven discipline pages are represented here as native method cards with case-grounded anchors. The case anchors (Meridian Network, Operation GRAY DAWN, the Caracas Question) sit in the pages as worked examples rather than gestures. The pillar is evidence of practiced work across the full discipline catalog, not only the few methods that headline the rest of the portfolio.
fields.source_utilization: Source use is organized by role: method authorities, domain evidence, technical background, implementation evidence, and claim limits. The page should show why each source matters; direct quotations and stronger factual claims still require exact passage anchors before publication.
fields.plan: Build out the cross-method-composition page that lets the eleven disciplines cite each other inside a single analytical question — an analyst working a CI question can pull in HS-style signal-detection moves; an FW forecasting question can use IC-cycle discipline. The composition page is what turns eleven adjacent pages into a single working method library.
fields.status: Status: The eleven discipline pages are represented here as native method cards with case-grounded anchors. The case anchors (Meridian Network, Operation GRAY DAWN, the Caracas Question) sit in the pages as worked examples rather than gestures. The pillar is evidence of practiced work across the full discipline catalog, not only the few methods that headline the rest of the portfolio.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: build out the cross-method-composition page that lets the eleven disciplines cite each other inside a single analytical question — an analyst working a CI question can pull in HS-style signal-detection moves; an FW forecasting question can use IC-cycle discipline. The composition page is what turns eleven adjacent pages into a single working method library.
fields.detailed_source_vision: A pillar of eleven discipline-specific analytical-methodology pages, each treating a recognised analytical lens (all-source, counter-intelligence, crisis management, counter-operations, cyber, defense / diplomacy / intelligence, emerging threats, foresight, homeland security, intelligence cycle, public-service analysis) as its own working page with its own case content. The pillar exists to demonstrate that the analyst can work across every named discipline rather than only the few that headline the rest of the portfolio.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: The page works by linking visible components, source roles, and next-step gates. The eleven discipline pages are represented here as native method cards with case-grounded anchors.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: A Georgetown Applied Intelligence methodology pillar that treats eleven analytical disciplines as distinct working surfaces.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It differentiates all-source, counterintelligence, crisis management, counter-operations, cyber, defense/diplomacy/intelligence, emerging-threats, foresight, homeland-security, intelligence-cycle, and public-service analysis by case anchor, source demand, method fit, output form, and cross-method composition. Meridian Network, Operation GRAY DAWN, the Caracas Question, and HS-style signal-detection moves function as worked anchors rather than empty labels.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It matters because experts need to see which method belongs to which kind of problem, when one discipline can borrow from another, and how eleven adjacent pages become a working method library rather than a list of course topics.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.

researcher_process_map.summary: A Georgetown Applied Intelligence methodology pillar that treats eleven analytical disciplines as distinct working surfaces.
researcher_process_map.final_use_case: It matters because experts need to see which method belongs to which kind of problem, when one discipline can borrow from another, and how eleven adjacent pages become a working method library rather than a list of course topics.
